signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.62 — Strait of Hormuz closure and UAE-Iran strikes drive crude prices +2.78% higher, introducing acute JPY downside via energy import dependency. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% (+300bp) carry sustains the bid despite VIX rising to 18.4. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 (crowded carry) remains vulnerable to BoJ verbal intervention if price tests 160.00, though geopolitical supply shocks currently outweigh safe-haven JPY demand.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.012 — G8 scorecard identifies a 1.046% spread between leader AUD and laggard JPY. RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75% (+360bp) carry advantage drives the divergence. Retail sentiment is 85% short, diverging from COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long), signaling institutional-retail friction. Retail capitulation risk supports further upside until the next BoJ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced).

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% at 18.5% extreme) vs RBNZ market bias for a hike. Specs are heavily fading the hawkish CB stance. If RBNZ signals or delivers a hike at the 27 May meeting, short-squeeze risk is elevated for the crowded spec short. NZD/USD at 0.5959 remains supported by the G8 strength (NZD +0.365% 1W avg) despite the USD 150bp carry advantage.

signals.title: USD/JPY vs US 10Y — Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation at +0.577 vs 252d norm of +0.092 (z=-1.91σ). The traditional link between JPY and US Treasury yields is tightening significantly as JPY becomes a pure carry-and-energy proxy. JPY safe-haven function is currently suppressed by the Strait of Hormuz disruption. Watch for a reversal in the 2s10s bear steepener (+51.0bp) to re-establish the yield-driven JPY bid.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1765 — Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) carry advantage favors USD, keeping upside structurally limited. EUR COT LF net short -21,011 reflects institutional caution ahead of US CPI data. Goldman Sachs shifting Fed cut forecast to December 2026 reinforces the USD duration bid. Watch 1.1800 resistance; failure to hold gains targets a return to 1.1700.
